programmes.show_description: How do they engineer the latest incarnation of the classic Royal Enfield Continental GT motorbike? Plus, how do 'art detectives' spot a forgery? S14, Ep5
How do they blend science and destruction at crash test centres? And how do they piece together the complex mechanism of a pocket sized umbrella? S14, Ep6
How do they extract iron ore from one of the most remote mines on the planet? And how do they make chandeliers that are fit for a queen? S14, Ep7
How do they forge the blades to crack open oyster shells? Plus, how do scientists in a lab grow the synthetic diamonds of the future? S14, Ep8
Jaguar Mark 2. Mike is firing on all cylinders when he tracks down a Jaguar Mark 2. But when Edd examines this iconic classic, will they find the job is bigger than their budget? S9, Ep9
Willys Jeep - USA. Mike heads to the USA to find the grandfather of four wheel drives, the Willys Jeep. But will major repairs and high transport costs stop them making a profit? S9, Ep10
Sam and Henry rescue interesting items from a scrapyard in Devon. The guys find some classic vehicles, including a neglected Jaguar Mark 1. S3, Ep8
Sam and Henry visit an incredible collection of Ford vehicles in Worcestershire. But the guys have their sights set on a 1960s Austin A40 Farina. S3, Ep9
Find out how inflatable water toys, couscous and modelling dough are made, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope. S2, Ep35
Find out how wind generators, PVC gloves, thermoformed glass and fire engines are made, as more everyday objects put under the microscope. S2, Ep36
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Steve and Dominic are tasked with repairing a clock that stood for decades above the entrance of a newsagent's. Sonnaz works on a wooden barber's chair. S4, Ep27
Lifelong Collectors. Drew Pritchard and Viki Knott travel across the country, visiting incredible collectors who have collections of unusual, rare and historic treasures. S20, Ep5
Trusted Hunting Grounds. Drew takes Al on a tour of trusted contacts, finding classic signage in a Kent railway shed, and an African colonial table at a Sussex antiques shop. S20, Ep6
The Poseidon Crew unearth two monster nuggets. The Gold Timers battle extreme heat and millions of flies as they desperately try to find gold. S5, Ep17
Gold thieves hit the Dust Devils' Siberia lease and steal a fortune in gold. Marcus and Linden persevere through extreme heat to unearth more nuggets. S5, Ep18
Izzie and Phil drive through a downpour in Oxfordshire to search of treasures. Will a long-lost saucer or designer brooch prove profitable the Harrogate auction? S22, Ep23
The Road To Riches, By Bus Or By Bike. In Sheffield, Angus helps a family find a new loving home for a collection of motorbikes left by their much-missed husband and dad. S2, Ep11
Steve and Dominic are tasked with repairing a clock that stood for decades above the entrance of a newsagent's. Sonnaz works on a wooden barber's chair. S4, Ep27
Sam and Henry are in Dorset to look around a collection of bikes, cars and tractors. The guys consider buying a pedestrian walk sign and a Yamaha FS1-SE. S3, Ep10
Henry and Sam are in Devon to visit a father and daughter who own a shed full of vintage Americana. They immediately take interest in a dusty Chicago police car. S4, Ep1
Henry Cole and the team take on a classic 1950s Massey Ferguson combine harvester in one of their biggest restorations yet.
In Scotland, Angus and the team clear out an attic filled with Ferrari memorabilia. How much will this incredible collection make at auction? S1, Ep5
In the seaside town of Filey, Angus and the team help a couple clear out their home. Angus sees potential in some vintage toys and boxes of old soap. S1, Ep6
Natasha Raskin and Paul Laidlaw are on the final leg of their road trip. With their last auction in Diss, who will make the biggest profit? S14, Ep15
James Braxton and Raj Bisram start the first leg of their trip in Bath. They travel to Bristol and Hungerford, before getting to the Isle of Wight. S14, Ep16
Theo And Sarah. Henry and Fuzz meet a lifelong biker with unique motorbikes. Fuzz discovers a 1950s step through scooter, but Henry prefers low-capacity sports bikes. S6, Ep5
Adam And Dan. Henry and stand-in searcher Guy are in Wiltshire visiting collectors Adam and Dan who have an assortment of trucks, tractors, and rare American cars. S6, Ep6
Lost Oils And Hidden Porcelain. Irita finds old Italian paintings and the rarest porcelain she's ever sold, and an aspiring antiques dealer finds out if he has an eye for a bargain. S3, Ep2
Time To Let Go. Angus finds thousands of comics that's one man's obsession, but his wife wants them gone. Plus, a man's poor health forces him to give up his passion for timepieces. S3, Ep14
How do they make cheddar, the world's most popular cheese? How do they breathe life into Rususci Anne - the plastic woman that teaches CPR? S14, Ep9
How do Ford produce the ubiquitous white van, the Transit? Plus, how do they ensure the Driza-bone coat lives up to its name? S14, Ep10
How do they make wood-burning stoves that bring warmth to the Norwegian winter? Plus, how did they build the synthesiser that changed music forever? S14, Ep11
How do they assemble the reinvented Morgan 3 Wheeler? Plus, how do they protect firefighters' hands from heat, flame and chemical attack? S14, Ep12
Nissan Skyline. Mike thinks he's on to a winner when he finds a budget Nissan Skyline supercar. Can Edd transform this modern classic into a customised drift racer? S9, Ep11
Triumph TR6. Mike tracks down a Triumph TR6 two-seater sports car; can Ed make it roadworthy without blowing their budget? Plus, Ed tests the latest water coolant technology. S9, Ep12
Sam and Henry are in Dorset to look around a collection of bikes, cars and tractors. The guys consider buying a pedestrian walk sign and a Yamaha FS1-SE. S3, Ep10
Henry and Sam are in Devon to visit a father and daughter who own a shed full of vintage Americana. They immediately take interest in a dusty Chicago police car. S4, Ep1
Find out how car radiators, filo pastry and cross-country skis are made, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope. S2, Ep37
Find out how packaged chicken, electric heaters, pulp containers and video games are produced, as more objects are put under the microscope. S2, Ep38
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Blue MacMurchie repairs a set of bagpipes that belonged to a soldier in the Royal Scots. And, Steve Fletcher works on a quirky tortoise-shaped bell. S4, Ep28
Eclectic Finds. On the road buying with Viki, Drew finds an extraordinary scratch-built model in Suffolk. In Kent, he snaps up a stunning garden statue of Mr Punch. S20, Ep7
Trade Tip Offs. Drew Pritchard and Alister Dryburgh visit three promising new antique trade contacts who they've been tipped off about. S20, Ep8
The Dust Devils are poised to smash the biggest season tally ever. The Gold Timers hit paydirt. The Gold Gypsies' gold dream could finally be over. S5, Ep19
The Gold Gypsies have more bad luck. The Victoria Diggers face a death in the family. The Poseidon Crew hope to continue their run of big gold. S5, Ep20
Izzie Balmer and Philip Serrell scour the shops of Staffordshire and Shropshire. Izzie discovers snake-related items whilst Phil is drawn to designer jewellery. S22, Ep24
Family Treasure Funds A New Home. Stuart hopes Angus can help him raise enough cash to adapt a house for his poorly wife's needs, so she can live her final years in comfort. S2, Ep12
Blue MacMurchie repairs a set of bagpipes that belonged to a soldier in the Royal Scots. And, Steve Fletcher works on a quirky tortoise-shaped bell. S4, Ep28
Henry and Sam look around an incredible Americana collection in Cornwall. After examining an array of classic motors, Sam springs a big surprise on Henry. S4, Ep2
Henry and Sam are invited to look around the workshops of the National Motorcycle Museum. The guys consider bidding on a 1926 Ariel and a 1927 BSA. S4, Ep3
Henry Cole and the team are in Austria for a unique winter project. They're looking for a vintage machine to restore for some fun on the slopes.
Angus and the team are in Derbyshire to help a widow clear out her home. They uncover a riding crop, a 19th century stool and a jewellery collection. S1, Ep7
In County Durham, Angus and the team clear out a huge aviation collection. Later, they help a retired policeman sell items from his four-bedroom home. S1, Ep8
After their first auction, only a couple of pounds separate James Braxton and Raj Bisram. They head to auction in Frome, Somerset. S14, Ep17
James Braxton and Raj Bisram continue their shopping spree through Somerset. It's the midway point and the antiques experts are neck and neck! S14, Ep18
Ross. Henry and Fuzz are in Surrey visiting classic cars and tractor collector Ross. Will they go for a gangster-style Rover or a 1920s Renault saloon? S6, Ep7
Nick And Hollie. Henry and Fuzz return to Surrey to visit collectors Nick and Hollie, who have an incredible array of cars including a 1921 Rolls Royce Silver Ghost. S6, Ep8
Brent and his son Cayden set off on an adventure to find highly valuable, crystalline gold. Plus, Sheryl and Simon hit it big on new ground. S10, Ep12
The Hunt Begins. Julia's first mission is a giant python in a living room. Then, she tries to catch a snake trapped in an air conditioning box. S1, Ep1
